away.starter_source: live ESPN roster/stat/injury APIs; starters inferred by live minutes + production

players.name: Alyssa Thomas
players.injury_comment: Thomas missed Friday's win over Portland due to a left calf injury but is likely to suit up Tuesday. The star forward has scored single-digit points in three of her last five appearances, during which she has averaged 11.8 points, 6.8 rebounds, 6.8 assists and 2.0 steals in 32.6 minutes per game.

players.name: Kahleah Copper
players.injury_comment: Copper sat out of Friday's matchup with the Fire due to a hip injury, but she'll have a chance to return Tuesday after being upgraded to questionable. The team should have a better idea on her status closer tipoff.

players.name: Sami Whitcomb
players.injury_comment: This is a tough blow for Whitcomb and the Mercury as she will now miss the first month or so of the WNBA season. Whitcomb served as a part-time starter during her first season in Phoenix last year, averaging 9.1 points, 2.6 rebounds and 2.5 assists in 23.9 minutes per game across 43 regular-season appearances (20 starts). Kiana Williams could see an uptick in minutes with this news.

home.starter_source: live ESPN roster/stat/injury APIs; starters inferred by live minutes + production

players.name: Iliana Rupert
players.injury_comment: Rupert signed a multi-year contract with Golden State during the offseason and missed the start of training camp due to an overseas commitment. Her status for the 2026 regular season now appears to be in jeopardy due to her pregnancy. The 24-year-old made 21 regular-season appearances (11 starts) for the Valkyries last year and averaged 9.3 points, 3.9 rebounds and 1.6 assists in 23.1 minutes per game. If Rupert is sidelined in 2026, Janelle Salaun, Kiah Stokes and Laeticia Amihere are candidates to see increased work.
